seo.title: 100 Vue (general) resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Vue (general) resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource provides a technical roadmap for implementing Vue 3 features in enterprise environments, focusing on the Composition API, TypeScript integration, and Pinia state management. It prioritizes practical patterns over theory to accelerate development in high-scale codebases.

items.title: Type-Safe defineProps
items.description: Utilize TypeScript interfaces within defineProps to enforce strict prop validation. Use the 'withDefaults' compiler macro to provide default values for optional props without losing type inference.

items.title: Reactive Destructuring with toRefs
items.description: When destructuring props or reactive objects, wrap the source in toRefs() to prevent losing reactivity. This is critical when passing individual prop values to composables.

items.title: Template Ref Typing
items.description: Define template refs using the generic ref syntax: const myInput = ref<HTMLInputElement | null>(null). This ensures proper IDE autocompletion and type checking for DOM methods.

items.title: ShallowRef for Large Datasets
items.description: Use shallowRef() instead of ref() for large arrays or complex objects that do not require deep reactivity. This reduces overhead by only tracking the top-level reference change.

items.title: Custom Composable Extraction
items.description: Encapsulate reusable logic into functions prefixed with 'use'. Ensure they return an object of refs to maintain reactivity when consumed by components.

items.title: Readonly State Protection
items.description: Wrap reactive state in readonly() before exporting it from a composable to prevent consumers from mutating state directly, enforcing a unidirectional data flow.

items.title: Provide/Inject with InjectionKey
items.description: Use InjectionKey symbols to provide type safety for dependency injection across deeply nested components, preventing naming collisions and runtime errors.

items.title: WatchEffect for Automatic Dependencies
items.description: Use watchEffect() when you need to track multiple reactive dependencies automatically without explicitly listing them, such as when syncing local state to localStorage.

items.title: Async Setup Handling
items.description: Leverage Top-Level Await in <script setup> when working with asynchronous data fetching. Ensure the parent component is wrapped in a <Suspense> boundary.

items.title: Computed Getters and Setters
items.description: Implement writable computed properties by providing an object with get() and set(value) methods, useful for wrapping v-model logic in child components.

items.title: Setup Store Syntax
items.description: Adopt the function-based 'Setup Store' syntax in Pinia to leverage the same mental model as the Composition API, making it easier to share logic between stores and components.

items.title: StoreToRefs for Destructuring
items.description: Apply storeToRefs() when extracting state or getters from a Pinia store to keep them reactive. Note that actions should be destructured directly from the store object.

items.title: Cross-Store Interaction
items.description: Import and instantiate one store inside another to handle cross-domain logic. Pinia handles the dependency tree automatically without circular dependency issues.

items.title: Store Action Subscriptions
items.description: Use store.$onAction() to implement global logging, analytics, or undo/redo functionality by intercepting store calls before or after they execute.

items.title: State Resetting in Setup Stores
items.description: Since Setup Stores don't have a built-in $reset, manually implement a reset function that returns the store state to its initial values to handle user logout scenarios.

items.title: Pinia Plugin Persistence
items.description: Integrate 'pinia-plugin-persistedstate' to automatically sync store state with localStorage or sessionStorage using a simple 'persist: true' configuration key.

items.title: Atomic State Updates with $patch
items.description: Use the $patch method to apply multiple changes to the state simultaneously, which can be more performant and easier to debug via Vue DevTools.

items.title: Testing Stores with createTestingPinia
items.description: Use @pinia/testing to create a fresh store instance for every unit test, allowing you to mock actions and verify state changes in isolation.

items.title: Composition-based Getters
items.description: Use computed() inside Setup Stores to create getters. This allows for complex derived state that caches results based on reactive dependencies.

items.title: Hydrating SSR State
items.description: Ensure the initial state from the server is passed to the Pinia instance on the client side to prevent hydration mismatch errors in Nuxt or custom SSR setups.

items.title: Vitest for SFC Unit Testing
items.description: Migrate from Jest to Vitest for faster unit testing of Single File Components (SFCs), taking advantage of Vite's native module resolution and fast HMR.

items.title: Vue Test Utils 'findComponent'
items.description: Use findComponent() to locate child components in the wrapper instead of querying DOM selectors, making tests more resilient to internal markup changes.

items.title: Testing Composables in Isolation
items.description: Test composables without a component by wrapping them in a dummy component or using a simple wrapper function to provide the necessary lifecycle context.

items.title: Volar Extension Setup
items.description: Disable the built-in VS Code 'TypeScript Language Features' for Vue projects and enable Volar's Take Over Mode to ensure accurate type checking in templates.

items.title: Component Code Splitting
items.description: Wrap heavy components (like charts or editors) in defineAsyncComponent() to split them into separate chunks, reducing the initial bundle size.

items.title: VueUse useIntersectionObserver
items.description: Implement lazy loading of components or infinite scrolling using the useIntersectionObserver composable from the VueUse library for a performance boost.

items.title: Teleport for Global UI
items.description: Use <Teleport to="body"> for modals and notifications to avoid CSS overflow or z-index issues caused by parent container constraints.

items.title: Optimizing v-for with Keys
items.description: Always use a unique identifier (like an ID) for :key in v-for loops. Avoid using array indices to prevent rendering glitches during list mutations.

items.title: Performance Profiling with DevTools
items.description: Use the Timeline tab in Vue DevTools to identify slow component renders and excessive reactivity triggers in complex views.

items.title: Unplugin-Vue-Components Integration
items.description: Configure unplugin-vue-components in Vite to automatically import UI library components (like PrimeVue or Element Plus) as they are used in templates.

pro_tips: Always use 'script setup' for new components to benefit from reduced boilerplate and better TypeScript support.
Prefer ref() over reactive() for all state declarations to maintain consistency and avoid issues with destructuring.
Utilize the 'v-memo' directive in large lists to skip re-rendering of sub-trees when the underlying data hasn't changed.
Keep composables focused on a single responsibility; compose multiple small composables into larger ones for complex features.
Use 'flush: post' in watchers if you need to access the DOM after it has been updated by Vue's reactivity system.
